summary.headline: Across all 10 ribbon tabs the clone's core typing/formatting/file path is solid, but the re-baseline confirms 64 severity-rated defects (0 S1, 19 S2, 28 S3, 14 S4, 3 S5) plus 64 honest-degrade stubs/fidelity gaps. The most dangerous cluster is editing-protection and silent data-loss on export: Restrict Editing's read-only mode does not block the programmatic ribbon write path, multi-author citation sources lose authors 2+ on edit, and mail-merge IF/ADDRESSBLOCK/GREETINGLINE/MERGEFIELD fields export invalid or switch-stripped OOXML. A second large cluster is paged-engine debt from the overlay-to-paged migration: View modes (Web/Draft/Outline/Side-to-Side), Split, and print/PDF still target the retired #editor node, and counts().pages reads the dead __pagination paginator so every Pages count (Info, Properties, Word Count) is stuck at 1. The richest engineered subsystems (TOC/footnotes/citations, tables, headers/footers, track-changes, the Editor pane) work but carry galleries that collapse to a single output (cover pages, style sets, themes' heading half) or dialogs whose advanced controls silently no-op. Most other gaps are honest, documented cloud/ML stubs (Dictate, Translate, SmartArt, Shapes, Researcher) that degrade gracefully. Versus Word: faithful for everyday authoring and docx round-trip, but not yet trustworthy under document protection, multi-record mail merge, multi-page page accounting, alternate view modes, or printing non-Letter geometry.

confirmed.evidence: commands.js:1881 setFontSize -> WC.PM.cmd('setFontSize', pt+'pt'). Fork clamps to defaults min:8 max:96 (font-size.js:57-58, minMax at :113). Word allows 1-1638pt. A typed combo value (comboCommit:1788 parseFloat) of e.g. 120 or 4 is SILENTLY clamped to 96/8 with no feedback. Confirms prior BUG-014 still live.
confirmed.note: Reachable via typed combo entry. Probe: setFontSize('120pt') then read back textStyle.fontSize == 96pt; COM oracle Word stores 120.

confirmed.evidence: commands.js:50 stepFont(1) walks SIZES list (commands.js:13: 8..72) then sticks at 72; also bounded by the 96pt fork clamp. Word's grow-font continues past 72 (80,90,...) up to 1638. Minor jump-list ceiling deviation.
confirmed.note: Matches Word's jump-list 8..72 but does not continue above 72. Cosmetic for normal use.

confirmed.evidence: commands.js:1937 changeCaseMenu -> commands.ts:53 changeCase. Sentence-case regex commands.ts:58 needs whitespace after terminator ('end.New' not recapitalized); toggle-case commands.ts:59 is locale-naive (Turkish dotless i, German eszett); no Shift+F3 cycle binding. Confirms prior BUG-025. Core upper/lower/caps are correct.
confirmed.note: Length-preserving single PM transaction (good). Edge-case fidelity only.

confirmed.evidence: commands.js:607-608 outlineMenu width presets pass color:'currentColor'. Export textOutline-translator.js:38 does String(o.color).replace(/^#/,'').toUpperCase() => 'CURRENTCOLOR', an invalid OOXML srgbClr w14:val. Word drops the invalid outline on open (data loss). Confirms prior BUG-051. (Outline Color... picker passes a real hex and is fine.)
confirmed.note: Only the width-preset items are broken; the explicit Outline Color path works. Probe: apply '1 pt outline', export, inspect w14:textOutline > w14:srgbClr val.

confirmed.evidence: commands.js:57 + 1637 colorMenu(node,'hilite') uses the FULL WC.colorPalette (theme + tints + standard + More Colors — util.js:109) instead of Word's fixed 15-keyword highlighter set. On export, highlight-translator.js:48 getDocxHighlightKeywordFromHex returns null for a non-keyword color -> falls back to w:shd (character shading) NOT w:highlight (lines 56-66). On reopen it is shading, not a highlight: different semantics + the highlighter button won't toggle it off. Confirms prior BUG-015.
confirmed.note: Picking any of the 15 Word keyword colors is fine; any other swatch silently becomes shading. The palette itself is wrong (should be the 15-keyword highlighter grid).

confirmed.evidence: commands.js:90/2081 sortDialog -> commands.ts:82 sortParagraphs. Type=Date maps to numeric parseFloat (commands.js:2095 numeric:type.value!=='Text'; commands.ts:102), so dates sort by leading number not chronologically. Confirms prior BUG-042. Text/Number sort correct; same-parent contiguity guard.
confirmed.note: Date sorting is the only defect; otherwise faithful single-transaction reorder.

confirmed.evidence: commands.js:93 + applyColor 'shade' (commands.js:1912-1916) ALWAYS writes paragraphProperties.shading (pPr/w:shd) regardless of selection. On a partial text (sub-paragraph) selection Word applies RUN shading (rPr/w:shd) to just the selected characters; the clone floods the entire paragraph. Confirms prior BUG-036.
confirmed.note: Whole-paragraph selection is correct; sub-paragraph selection over-applies. Probe: select 2 words, apply shading, check pPr vs rPr shd.

confirmed.evidence: commands.js:578 H.dictate -> WC.toast("Dictate isn't available in this clone"); dropdown commands.js:1654 same. feasible:'no' in ribbon-data. No Web Speech wiring in current handler (docs/HOME_TAB.md 'Web Speech API' claim is stale).
confirmed.note: Honest-degrade toast. Speech-to-text out of scope.

confirmed.evidence: commands.js:579 H.sensitivity -> WC.toast("Sensitivity labels aren't available in this clone"); dropdown commands.js:1655 same toast. (sensitivityMenu at commands.js:696 + WC.setSensitivity exist but are NOT wired to H.sensitivity/the dropdown — dead path.) ribbon-data feasible:'no'. docs/HOME_TAB.md 'label bar + round-trips' claim is stale.

confirmed.evidence: commands.js:581/705 addInsMenu -> addInsStoreDialog (dialogs of sample store entries; 'Add' toasts 'runtime not available'). No Office.js runtime. ribbon-data feasible:'no'.
confirmed.note: Mock store UI only. Honest-degrade.

confirmed.evidence: commands.js:583 H.reuseFiles -> WC.toast('Reuse Files surfaces content from your cloud/OneDrive documents — not available in this local clone.'). feasible:'no'.
confirmed.note: M365 cloud feature; honest-degrade toast.

confirmed.control: Underline dropdown (styles / More Underlines / Underline Color)
confirmed.evidence: commands.js:2127 underlineMenu offers ONLY style sub-items (Single/Double/Dotted/Dashed/Wavy via setMark underline underlineType). The ribbon-data items list 'More Underlines...' and 'Underline Color' (ribbon-data.js:156-160) are NOT rendered — no color picker, no More Underlines entry. Underline color is unreachable from the Home dropdown.
confirmed.note: Underline color is reachable nowhere on Home; Font dialog also has no underline-color control. Niche.

confirmed.evidence: dialogs.js:376 D.font. Applies family/size/style/color/underline-style/strike/sub-sup as ONE PM chain (good). BUT Small caps, All caps, Character Scale, Spacing (Expanded/Condensed), Position (Raised/Lowered) only update the PREVIEW; on OK (dialogs.js:460-461) if any are set it calls pm.notifyBlocked('Caps and Advanced font effects') and drops them — no w:smallCaps/w:caps/w:spacing/w:position/w:w emitted. Confirms prior BUG-035.
confirmed.note: Dialog presents controls that silently no-op + toast. Core character formatting works; advanced/caps tab is inert.

confirmed.evidence: commands.js:83 toggles #pm-editor.show-marks class; CSS editor.css:490 renders ONLY a pilcrow on p::after. No space dots (·), tab arrows, or line-break/anchor marks that Word's Show/Hide also draws. In the paged PresentationEditor the body is painted per-page sheets, so whether p::after even matches the painted DOM is unverified.
confirmed.note: Live check: toggle on a doc with spaces/tabs/breaks across pages and confirm marks render at all in paged paint. Latch state via state-sync (formattingMarks).

confirmed.control: Select (Select All / Select Objects / Select Similar Formatting / Selection Pane)
confirmed.evidence: commands.js:103/2064 selectMenu: Select All (selectAll) + Select All Text With Similar Formatting (selectSimilarFormatting) work; Select Objects -> dSetSelect (draw). BUT 'Selection Pane...' (commands.js:2071) only toasts 'arrives with the Draw engine re-host (slice 10)' — not implemented.
confirmed.note: Selection Pane (object list) is the only missing item; the rest work. Niche.

confirmed.evidence: commands.js:580 H.editor -> WC.Dialogs.editorPane (dialogs.js:595 exists — spellcheck toggle + summary). ribbon-data feasible:'no' but a pane is wired. Not a real grammar/clarity engine; summary/proofing depth limited.
confirmed.note: Pane opens (more than a toast) but proofing intelligence is shallow vs Word's Editor. Probe what the pane actually reports.

confirmed.evidence: commands.js:435 H.coverPage -> insert-features.js:24-37 coverPageMenu lists 6 designs (Banded/Facet/Filigree/Ion/Motion/Retrospect) each calling Insert.insertCover(t) -> WC.PM.xeCoverPage(t.name). bridge/insert-exotica.ts:69 xeCoverPage(name) uses `name` ONLY as the title-paragraph string (line 78 `title = name`); the documentPartObject content (lines 88-92) is a fixed 3-paragraph stub (title bold / [Subtitle] / [Author Name] - year) regardless of which design was chosen. Word inserts 6 visually distinct fully-formatted cover layouts. The ribbon-data gallery/'More Cover Pages'/'Save Selection' items are also not individually wired. Remove Current Cover Page works (xeRemoveCoverPage deletes the first documentPartObject).
confirmed.note: All 6 gallery designs collapse to ONE generic 3-paragraph block (design name ignored). Re-test of prior BUG-009 — STILL PRESENT on the exotica bridge.

confirmed.evidence: commands.js:436/1663 -> WC.Insert.shapesMenu (insert-features.js:104) renders the full categorized gallery. insert-features.js:118-122 Insert.insertShape() = WC.toast('Inserting ... shapes isn't available on the new engine yet.') with NO PM verb. A VectorShape fork extension IS registered (extensions/index.js:52,229) so a real path is feasible, but the bridge never calls it. Word inserts a real DrawingML auto-shape (wps:sp). Re-test of prior catalogued S3 'Shapes stub'.
confirmed.note: STUB BEHIND A COMPLETE-LOOKING PICKER (most misleading control on the tab). Full shape gallery (8 categories + SVG previews) renders, but clicking ANY shape only toasts 'not available on the new engine yet' — zero document change.

confirmed.evidence: commands.js:438 H.smartart -> WC.Insert.smartArtMenu (insert-features.js:146) renders List/Process/Cycle/Hierarchy -> Insert.insertSmartArt(k) -> WC.PM.xeSmartArt(). bridge/insert-exotica.ts:179 xeSmartArt = toast('SmartArt ... needs a diagram subsystem'); returns true (no mutation). Word inserts a real dgm: diagram. INSERT_TAB.md falsely claims SmartArt is 'editable' (stale doc).
confirmed.note: STUB: button menu renders 4 layouts, but every layout calls xeSmartArt() which is a no-op toast — no diagram inserted.

confirmed.evidence: commands.js:439 H.chart -> WC.Insert.chartDialog (insert-features.js:155); OK onClick -> WC.PM.xeChart() (bridge/insert-exotica.ts:178 = toast, no mutation). Insert.chartSVG (insert-features.js:168) builds SVG but is DEAD (never inserted — the legacy WC.Editor inline-SVG path was removed). Fork extensions/chart/chart.js:18 is a Node with parseDOM/renderDOM but NO addCommands/insertChart, so imported charts render but new charts can't be created. INSERT_TAB.md falsely claims 'Chart from entered data -> inline SVG' (stale).
confirmed.note: STUB: the Insert Chart dialog (type + data textarea) renders and even computes an SVG locally, but OK calls xeChart() which is a no-op toast — nothing is inserted. The 'chart' fork extension is render/import-only (no insertChart command).

confirmed.evidence: commands.js:442 H.myAddIns -> addInsMenu(node). feasible:'no' ribbon-data:764. No document/runtime effect. Acceptable honest degrade.
confirmed.note: Honest stub: routes to addInsMenu (no installed add-ins, no Office.js runtime).

confirmed.evidence: commands.js:481 H.signatureLine -> WC.Insert.signatureLine (insert-features.js:219) shows a dialog; OK -> WC.PM.xeSignatureLine() (bridge/insert-exotica.ts:181 = toast 'Signature lines need the signature-provider subsystem', no mutation, returns true). Word inserts a real signature-line content control. feasible:'no' ribbon-data.
confirmed.note: STUB: a Signature Setup dialog (signer/title) renders, but OK calls xeSignatureLine() which is a no-op toast — no signature-line object is inserted.

confirmed.evidence: commands.js:482 H.dateTime / dispatch 1676 -> WC.Insert.dateTimeDialog (insert-features.js:186). The 'Update automatically' checkbox `upd` (line 190) is BUILT but never read; OK onClick (line 193) always calls WC.PM.xeDateTime(fmt) -> bridge/insert-exotica.ts:118 inserts a DATE field unconditionally. Word: unchecked = static text snapshot, checked = field. Also verify the format list (6 entries, line 188) aligns with the OK mapping array (6 entries, line 193).
confirmed.note: Dialog inserts a REAL DATE field, but the 'Update automatically' checkbox is IGNORED — it always inserts an updating field. Word inserts STATIC text when the box is unchecked (the dialog's default). Minor wrong-behavior.

confirmed.evidence: commands.js:1659 dropdown -> WC.Insert.tableMenu (insert-features.js:47). Grid cells -> Insert.buildTable -> WC.PM.insertTable({rows,cols}) (bridge/table.ts:34, real chain().insertTable). 'Insert Table...' -> dialogs.js:11 (grid + numeric rows/cols, 1-1000 validated). 'Draw Table' (insert-features.js:64) drag-to-size -> buildTable. 'Quick Tables' -> fixed-size tables. STUBS: 'Convert Text to Table...' (insert-features.js:44 -> toast only) and 'Excel Spreadsheet' (insert-features.js:83 -> toast 'needs a host runtime'). Word converts selected text into a table and embeds an OLE Excel sheet.
confirmed.note: Live grid + Insert Table dialog + Draw Table + Quick Tables all build real tables via WC.PM.insertTable. Convert Text to Table and Excel Spreadsheet are stubs (toasts).

confirmed.evidence: commands.js:390 H.pictures -> window.wordAPI.pickImage() -> insertPictureFromDataUrl -> WC.PM.insertImage (bridge/insert.ts:45 real setImage with rId bypass to keep data-url, natural-size clamp, 50MB guard). The DROPDOWN path commands.js:1662 -> picturesMenu (commands.js:488) offers only 'This Device' + 'Online Pictures' (toast 'needs an image search backend'). ribbon-data lists 3 items but Stock Images isn't surfaced. SVG/non-PNG data-urls fall back to 480x360 default (readImageDimensionsFromDataUri is PNG-only, insert.ts:91-95) — live-check SVG picture sizing.
confirmed.note: This Device inserts a real image node at natural-size clamped to column. Stock Images / Online Pictures are not implemented (toast). Dropdown items in ribbon-data not all wired to the live picturesMenu.

confirmed.evidence: commands.js:443 H.onlineVideo -> WC.Insert.onlineVideoDialog (insert-features.js:314) -> Insert.insertVideoThumbnail (insert-features.js:326) builds an SVG data-url poster and inserts via insertPictureFromDataUrl; the URL is kept in alt text. (bridge xeOnlineVideo at insert-exotica.ts:169 inserts only a hyperlink and is NOT the live path — the JS poster path wins.) Word embeds a playable video frame; here it is a static poster image. Round-trips as an image, not a video object.
confirmed.note: Inserts a real, self-contained SVG video-poster image (play button + host + URL) as a normal picture; embedded playback is sandbox-blocked. NOT a real web-video embed. Honest, useful degrade.

confirmed.evidence: commands.js:468 H.header / dispatch 1665 -> headerFooterDialog('header') (commands.js:450) = a textarea bound to WC.PM.getHeaderText/setHeaderText (bridge/header-footer.ts:133-136, story-runtime commit). header in ENGINE_READY (index.ts:179) so not isBlocked despite header-footer being DEFERRED. The 5 ribbon-data items (Built-in gallery / More Headers / Edit Header / Remove Header / Save Selection) are NOT individually wired. Live confirm: header repeats on every paged sheet.
confirmed.note: Dropdown opens a minimal Edit Header text dialog (single textarea) that round-trips header TEXT to word/headerN.xml + sectPr ref (Word-COM-validated, 002). NOT blocked anymore. GAPS: no built-in gallery, no separate Remove, no Save-Selection — the 5 ribbon-data items collapse into one text dialog. On-page editing exists via the Header & Footer Tools contextual tab, not from this dropdown.

confirmed.evidence: commands.js:469 H.footer / dispatch 1666 -> headerFooterDialog('footer') -> WC.PM.getFooterText/setFooterText (bridge/header-footer.ts:134-136). footer in ENGINE_READY (index.ts:179). The 5 ribbon-data items collapse to one Edit dialog (same pattern as Header). Live confirm: footer repeats per page.
confirmed.note: Same as Header: minimal Edit Footer text dialog round-tripping footer TEXT to word/footerN.xml + sectPr (002, Word-COM-validated). No built-in gallery / explicit Remove / Save-Selection wiring.

confirmed.evidence: commands.js:472 H.pageNumber flyout: Top/Bottom/Current -> WC.PM.insertPageNumber({position}); Remove -> WC.PM.removePageNumbers. bridge/header-footer.ts:225-251 insertPageNumber resolves the slot story (isHeaderOrFooter editor) and story.doc.fields.insert({instruction:'PAGE',mode:'raw'}). removePageNumbers (header-footer.ts:281) clears ONLY bands holding a PAGE field. MISSING flyout items vs ribbon-data:877-883: 'Page Margins' and 'Format Page Numbers...'. In-app paint shows '0' for a fresh field (FEATURES.md:95) — needs live confirm vs Word + COM oracle (wdFieldPage=33).
confirmed.note: Flyout (Top/Bottom/Current/Remove) inserts a REAL OOXML PAGE field into header/footer via the slot story editor (002 P3). KNOWN FORK-GATED GAP: a freshly-inserted field paints '0' in-app (the paged PE doesn't resolve a fresh field); real Word resolves per page. Also missing vs ribbon-data: 'Page Margins' position and 'Format Page Numbers...' (numbering format / start-at).

confirmed.evidence: commands.js:483 H.object / dispatch 1674 -> WC.Insert.objectMenu (insert-features.js:302): 'Text from File...' -> Insert.textFromFile (insert-features.js:308) -> window.wordAPI.open() -> WC.PM.pasteHTMLString(WC.Files.sanitize(html)). 'Object... (Create New)' -> toast 'Embedding OLE objects ... needs a host runtime'. Word embeds an OLE object; Text from File inserts file contents (matches).
confirmed.note: 'Text from File...' WORKS (opens a file, sanitizes + pastes its HTML into the doc). 'Object... (Create New)' is a no-op toast (no OLE embedding).

confirmed.evidence: commands.js:426 H.equation / dispatch 1673 -> WC.Dialogs.equation (dialogs.js:85, presets + free text) and equationMenu (commands.js:507, built-in gallery) -> WC.PM.insertEquation (bridge/insert.ts:177-217). DELIBERATE DEVIATION (insert.ts:181): equation = Cambria Math italic styled text, not m:oMath — round-trips as styled text, so Word does NOT see an equation object. Alt+= (ribbon-data:990) is absent from the app.js chord map (only Ctrl+= zoom at app.js:93; pure Alt+= returns early at app.js:78). 'Ink Equation...' / 'Save Selection...' (ribbon-data:995-996) not wired.
confirmed.note: Built-in gallery + Insert New Equation insert Unicode equation TEXT styled Cambria Math + italic — NOT real OOXML <m:oMath>. Documented deviation. Alt+= shortcut (advertised in tooltip) is NOT wired. Ink Equation / Save-Selection items not wired.

confirmed.evidence: Two render paths. (a) In-ribbon gallery (ribbon.js:260-289 renderPensGallery): a tile's active highlight reads draw.pen && draw.pen.id === pen.id (line 271). WC.Draw.pen is NEVER reassigned anywhere (grep for 'WC.Draw.pen =' / 'Draw.pen =' returns no matches); it stays PENS[0]. So only the first 'Pen' tile ever shows active, regardless of the pen actually selected via dSetPen (draw.ts:120 only mutates the bridge drawState.pen, not WC.Draw.pen). (b) Flyout (commands.js:761 pensMenu) lists pens and calls dSetPen but also never updates WC.Draw.pen. This is prior BUG-019's pen-select half, still live. Also: the tooltip promises 'click again to set Thickness/Color/Effects' — there is no per-pen options flyout (tile click just toggles drawing).
confirmed.note: Pen selection works functionally (dSetPen sets the bridge pen), but the active-pen highlight is dead (always 'Pen' tile) and the 2nd-click pen-options (thickness/color/effects) flyout is missing. Wrong pressed-state UI = S3 fidelity.

confirmed.evidence: commands.js:740 H.drawing = () => { WC.PM.dSetDrawing(!WC.PM.dIsDrawing()); }. The bridge toggle works (draw.ts:108 flips drawState.on + syncs the overlay capture binding). BUG: the ribbon button gets NO pressed/latched state — 'drawing' is absent from ribbon.js TOGGLE_MAP (ll.35-40) and registers no state rule (grep of registerRibbonRule shows none for Draw), and H.drawing ignores the node so it never toggles '.toggled'. So the Drawing button never visually latches even though ink mode is on. Also commands.js:771 pensMenu reads WC.Draw.enabled to label 'Start/Stop Drawing', but WC.Draw.enabled is never set (always false) so it always says 'Start Drawing'. This is the drawing-toggle half of prior BUG-019, still live (ribbon.js:271/278 era, now commands.js:740/771).
confirmed.note: Ink mode toggles correctly under the hood, but the button never shows pressed-state and the menu label is stuck on 'Start Drawing' (WC.Draw.enabled never set). Wrong UI state = S3.

confirmed.evidence: commands.js:757 H.inkToShape = () => WC.toast('Ink-to-Shape recognition is a handwriting/shape ML feature — not implemented.'). The bridge dInkToShape (draw.ts:150) also only toasts. Declared as type:'toggle' in ribbon-data (line 1113) but never latches (no state rule). Word converts drawn ink into clean geometric shapes (ML). Honest-degrade stub; matches DRAW_TAB.md ❌.
confirmed.note: Documented ML stub (toast only). Niche; not feasible without shape-recognition. Toggle never latches.

confirmed.evidence: commands.js:758 H.inkToMath = () => WC.toast('Ink-to-Math ... is not implemented.'). Bridge dInkToMath (draw.ts:151) toasts only. Word opens an ink-math input panel that recognizes a handwritten equation into a typeset OMML equation. Honest-degrade stub; matches DRAW_TAB.md ❌.
confirmed.note: Documented ML stub (toast only). Niche; equation-recognition not feasible for a clone.

confirmed.evidence: commands.js:754 H.selectObjects -> WC.PM.dSetSelect() (draw.ts:118) sets drawState.tool='select'. The selection is implemented ONLY over ink: ink-overlay.ts selectAt() (ll.309-315) hit-tests strokes.find on '.pm-ink-stroke' and adds a 'sel' class; Delete/Backspace removes the underlying vectorShape node (onKey ll.459-463). There is NO marquee/drag-rectangle, NO move/resize of selected objects, and it cannot select non-ink drawing objects (images, shapes, canvas, text boxes). Word's Select Objects gives a pointer that marquee-selects ALL floating objects and lets you move/resize them. Confirms prior BUG-065 against current code (ink-overlay.ts:309).
confirmed.note: Ink-only single-stroke select; no marquee, no move/resize, no non-ink objects. needsLive to confirm the on-screen select/move behavior.

confirmed.evidence: commands.js:755 H.lassoSelect -> WC.PM.dSetLasso() (draw.ts:119). ink-overlay.ts finishLasso() (ll.316-331) draws a freeform loop and selects strokes only if inside/total > 0.6 (line 328) — strokes <60% enclosed are dropped, whereas Word selects any stroke the loop touches/encloses. Ink-only (hit-tests '.pm-ink-stroke'), delete-only (no move). Confirms prior BUG-063 (ink-overlay.ts:328).
confirmed.note: Lasso works for ink but uses a 60%-enclosure heuristic (drops partial strokes) and is ink-only/delete-only. Word lasso is more permissive and object-agnostic.

confirmed.evidence: commands.js:742-751 H.addPen flyout adds Pen/Pencil/Highlighter/Action Pen presets + a Custom Pen dialog (addPenDialog ll.775-784: color picker + thickness slider). New pens push to WC.Draw.customPens and call dSetPen + _renderPens. Functional, but: (1) the custom-pen dialog hardcodes opacity:1 (commands.js:781) with no opacity/transparency control (Word's pen has a transparency slider) — prior BUG-019d; (2) added pens are session-only (customPens is an in-memory array, not persisted); (3) Action Pen is added as a plain pen (color #156082, no 'action pen' behavior).
confirmed.note: Add Pen works for adding presets/custom pens to the gallery; missing opacity control, no persistence, Action Pen is decorative.

confirmed.evidence: commands.js:752 H.drawWithTrackpad = (c, node) => { if (node) node.classList.toggle('toggled'); WC.toast('Mouse/trackpad input is used for drawing in this clone.'); }. It only toggles a local CSS class + toasts; it does not change any input mode (mouse/pointer is always used regardless). The toggled state is local-only and not synced (not in ribbon.js TOGGLE_MAP, no state rule), so it can drift from reality. Functionally a no-op beyond a cosmetic latch. (On Windows real Word this control is 'Draw with Touch'.)
confirmed.note: Cosmetic toggle + toast; no behavioral effect (pointer input is always on). Niche/honest-degrade.

confirmed.evidence: commands.js:756 H.drawingCanvas -> WC.PM.dInsertCanvas() (draw.ts:63-105) inserts a vectorShape{kind:'rect'} 480x240 with a real a:prstGeom rect + a:ln border + noFill via wp:INLINE (drawingContent ll.71-97). It renders as an SVG rect (VectorShapeView createElement, line 353) and exports as a real DrawingML rect. BUT it is NOT a Word drawing canvas: (1) Word's canvas is a wpc:wpc bounded group you draw shapes INSIDE; this is a plain inline rect — DRAW_TAB.md:34 confirms real wpc:wpc is deferred. (2) It is inserted INLINE (wp:inline), so it does not float like Word's canvas. (3) The ink overlay does not clip strokes to the canvas bounds — ink drawn 'inside' is not parented/grouped to the canvas, so moving the canvas would not move the ink. Round-trips to Word as a rectangle, not a canvas object.
confirmed.note: Inserts a real bordered rect, not a functional wpc:wpc drawing canvas; inline (non-floating); ink isn't grouped/clipped to it. needsRuntime to confirm the export OOXML (rect vs canvas) and needsLive for the on-page render.

confirmed.evidence: commands.js:759 H.inkReplay -> WC.PM.dReplay() (draw.ts:126) -> overlay.replay() (ink-overlay.ts:416-441): animates each rendered stroke via stroke-dashoffset in DOM order, toasting 'Replaying ink…' / 'No ink to replay.'. It is a one-shot animation in document order — there is NO pause/play/scrub timeline control and it does NOT use real stroke timestamps (ink has no captured time, so 'order they were drawn' is approximated by node/DOM order). DRAW_TAB.md:26 marks Ink Replay ❌; current code is actually a basic animation (better than stub) but lacks the Replay player UI.
confirmed.note: Basic dashoffset replay animation exists (more than the doc's ❌ claim) but no play/pause/scrub UI and no real stroke-time ordering. Niche.

confirmed.evidence: commands.js:787 H.themes -> WC.PM.deApplyTheme -> design.ts:76 deApplyTheme -> redefine(themeUpdates(...)) (design.ts:50-63 builds updates ONLY for ['Title','Subtitle','Heading1','Heading2','Heading3','Normal']) -> fork command linked-styles.js:121 redefineNamedStyles. The export pass (linked-styles.js:164-181) deletes asciiTheme/hAnsiTheme bindings ONLY for the styleIds in `updates`. The linked '*Char' character styles (e.g. Heading1Char) are NOT in `updates`, so they keep w:asciiTheme="majorHAnsi". Word resolves run fonts from the linked Char style in preference to the literal, so headings still render in the OLD theme's major font (Aptos Display) while body becomes the new font. Body half works, heading half doesn't.
confirmed.note: Re-test of BUG-011 (was Word-confirmed). Code path UNCHANGED on the paged engine: themeUpdates() never enumerates the linked *Char styles, and redefineNamedStyles only strips theme bindings on the passed styleIds. Fix: also redefine/strip the linked Char styles' asciiTheme on theme apply. Needs runtime+COM oracle (read-word-props on a saved theme2.docx heading) to re-confirm the heading-font asymmetry.

confirmed.evidence: commands.js:787 isActive=(t)=>firstFont(t.body)===currentDocFont(); commands.js:800 currentDocFont() reads getComputedStyle(documentElement)['--doc-font']. --doc-font is defined statically only in base.css:52 ('Aptos',Calibri,...) and is NEVER set by any apply path (grep for setProperty('--doc-font') across src/renderer returns zero writes; deApplyTheme/deApplyFonts in design.ts redefine named styles + docDefaults but do not touch --doc-font). So currentDocFont() always returns 'Aptos' -> the gallery checkmark is permanently stuck on the Office theme regardless of which theme is applied.

So both paths capture the FULL app chrome: title bar, ribbon, status bar, the gray #pages background and inter-page gap bands. Word prints only the document pages. Re-confirmed against current paged engine; matches prior BUG-030.
confirmed.note: Prior BUG-030 [S3] re-validated and arguably worse than S3 (full chrome capture). needsLive to see exact captured raster. Compounded by the geometry hardcode below.

confirmed.evidence: main.js:448-452 printToPDF hardcodes `pageSize:'Letter'` and `margins:{marginType:'none'}` unconditionally. It ignores the document's actual page setup (A4/Legal/custom size, Landscape orientation, real margins) — all of which the clone now supports via Layout tab + the paged engine. A Landscape or A4 doc exports to a portrait Letter PDF with the wrong clipping. Word's PDF export honors the document page setup.
confirmed.note: Independent of BUG-030 (chrome capture). Even with a print stylesheet, the page box would be wrong for non-Letter/non-portrait docs.

confirmed.control: Print pane — settings controls (Copies / Printer / Pages / Orientation / Paper size / Export to PDF instead)
confirmed.evidence: backstage.js:120-127 builds <input>/<select> rows for Copies, Printer, Pages range, Orientation, Paper size, but NONE are read on print: printBtn (line 117-118) calls WC.Files.print() with no args, and main.js:460 doc:print takes no options — webContents.print() shows the OS print dialog where the user re-picks everything. So Copies/Printer/Pages/Orientation/Paper-size in the backstage are pure decoration with no effect on the print job. (The 'Export to PDF instead' button at line 125-126 DOES work → exportPdf.) Word's Print backstage settings drive the actual job.
confirmed.note: UI present, inert. Pages-range in particular is meaningful in Word; here it's ignored.

confirmed.evidence: commands.js:1562 propertiesDialog rows include ['Pages', c.pages] and dialogs.js:266 Word Count rows include ['Pages', c.pages]; both call WC.PM.counts() which has the dead __pagination read (io.ts:49). Same root cause as the Info-pane Pages bug — always 1. Listed separately since these are distinct surfaces a user sees.
confirmed.note: Fixing counts().pages once repairs Info pane, Properties dialog, and Word Count simultaneously.

confirmed.evidence: backstage.js:49 `if (pane==='share') { WC.toast('Sharing requires a cloud backend — not implemented.'); return; }`; ribbon.js:117 the top-right Share pill shows the same toast. Honest stub — Word's Share needs OneDrive/cloud co-authoring. No data effect.
confirmed.note: Matches NOT_IMPLEMENTED.md co-authoring bucket.

confirmed.evidence: backstage.js:66-80 pane_feedback renders 3 cards (like/dislike/suggestion) with a textarea + Submit that just toasts 'Thanks for your feedback!' and clears the box (line 73) — nothing is sent or stored. Help → Feedback opens it (commands.js:1571). Honest no-backend stub.
confirmed.note: Word's Feedback files to Microsoft; here it's a UI-only form. Acceptable clone behavior.

confirmed.evidence: backstage.js:164-171 pane_options shows a READ-ONLY key/value list (Theme, Default font, Page size, Margins, Editor engine, File round-trip) and a note (:170) that 'the full multi-tab Word Options dialog (Proofing, AutoCorrect, Advanced, Customize Ribbon, etc.) is not implemented.' None of the values are editable. Word's Options is a large multi-tab dialog that changes real behavior (e.g. AutoCorrect, default font, proofing). Here it's purely informational.
confirmed.note: Honest stub. P2 because some sub-settings (default font, AutoCorrect) have real document impact users expect. Building it is a large UI job.

confirmed.evidence: backstage.js:130-133 renders a single .sheet with sheet.innerHTML = WC.PM.getHTML() (the whole body HTML crammed into one 360x466px box, backstage.css:95 has overflow:hidden). It is NOT paginated — a multi-page doc shows only the top of page 1, clipped. Word's print preview shows real paginated pages with prev/next navigation and a page counter. The paged engine could supply per-page sheets but the preview ignores it.
confirmed.note: Cosmetic-ish but misrepresents what will print; related to the missing pagination in counts/print.

confirmed.evidence: Word lets you pin recent items and right-click → 'Clear unpinned Documents'. The backend recent:clear handler EXISTS (main.js:280, preload.js:33 wordAPI.recent.clear) but no UI ever calls it (Grep: recent.clear has no renderer caller) and there is no pin concept in recent-files.json or the row markup (backstage.js:201-207). Niche.
confirmed.note: Backend is half-wired (clear exists, pin doesn't). Low priority.

confirmed.control: Info pane — Protect Document / Inspect Document / Version History
confirmed.evidence: backstage.js:110 explicitly states these three are 'part of Word but are not implemented in this clone (see docs/NOT_IMPLEMENTED.md)'. They are absent — no buttons, no dialogs. Honestly disclosed; require document protection / metadata-inspection / version backends. Niche for a clone.
confirmed.note: Documented stub, matches NOT_IMPLEMENTED.md. P3.

buckets.name: Read-only / editing protection enforcement
buckets.rationale: Single highest-risk protection defect: setEditable(false) only gates user DOM input, not the programmatic WC.PM/H[cmd] ribbon write path, so formatting, tr.insertText and addComment still mutate a 'read-only' doc. A spec must add an editable-aware guard at the single document-write seam (bridge/index.ts isBlocked) so ALL programmatic writes are rejected under protection. Coherent, self-contained, and a trust/protection promise that is currently broken.

buckets.name: Mail-merge field-code export fidelity
buckets.rationale: All of these are one OOXML-correctness problem: dialogs collect parameters that never reach the parameterless bridge verbs, so IF exports human operator labels + literal fields (non-evaluating), ADDRESSBLOCK/GREETINGLINE drop \f/\c/\e/\l switches, MERGEFIELD leaves space-containing names unquoted (truncates columns), Finish&Merge/Directory drop the per-record page break (paged importer can't parse the manual-break div) and ignore mergeType, the CSV import keeps the UTF-8 BOM, and preview won't resolve composite/rule fields. A single spec fixes the field-instruction builders + bridge parameter threading + per-record pageBreakBefore. Wrong-output data path.

buckets.name: Print & PDF output fidelity (chrome + page geometry)
buckets.rationale: Printing/exporting captures the full app chrome (no @media print / @page stylesheet exists) AND hardcodes pageSize:'Letter' + margins:none, so any A4/Legal/Landscape/custom doc exports clipped to portrait Letter with ribbon+statusbar baked in — the printed/PDF artifact is the user's deliverable, so this is effectively data-loss-grade. One spec adds a print stylesheet scoped to .superdoc-page, routes the backstage Copies/Pages/Orientation/Paper settings into the job, drives printToPDF from real section geometry, and paginates the preview.

buckets.name: Citation source multi-author data loss + placeholders
controls: References › Insert Citation (Add New Source / Add New Placeholder)
buckets.rationale: BUG-047 S2 data loss: the Add Source dialog captures a single author string and Source Manager Edit prefills authors[0] then Object.assign-overwrites the fork's multi-author array, so editing any multi-author source destroys authors 2+. Same surface also stubs Add New Placeholder and lacks the Master/Current two-pane manager. A single spec reworks the author model end-to-end (dialog → buildSourceFields → fork update) to preserve all authors, plus placeholders. Irreversible data loss = top priority.

buckets.name: Paged page-count accounting (counts().pages)
buckets.rationale: io.ts counts() reads w.WC.PM.__pagination.pageCount — the overlay paginator retired in feature 008 — so it always falls back to 1 under the paged engine; the correct value is WC.PM.coords.getPageCount(). One ~one-line routing fix simultaneously repairs the Info pane, Properties dialog and Word Count. Core-broken numeric correctness, already tracked as task_3436e431.

buckets.name: Paged view modes & window layout (dead-#editor CSS)
buckets.rationale: Identical root cause from the overlay→paged migration: all view-mode/movement/split CSS still targets the retired #editor id, so Web/Draft/Outline/Side-to-Side/Split produce no visible change in the paged engine (and Outline additionally sets the status bar to 'Print'). One spec re-points the view-mode CSS/engine hooks to #pm-editor/.superdoc-page (or switches the engine layout mode) and fixes the Outline view-state literal. Grouped because they share the fix and the file.

buckets.name: Floating-object arrange completeness (Position/Align/Wrap/Rotate)
controls: Layout › Position (vertical anchor; More Layout Options)
Layout › Align (10 of 13 items missing; to-Page/Margin; distribute)
Layout › Wrap Text (Edit Wrap Points; Move-with-Text/Fix-Position; More Layout Options)
Layout › Rotate (More Rotation Options arbitrary angle)
buckets.rationale: The Phase-4/012 frames work shipped the cores but each arrange control exposes only a subset of Word's menu: Position collapses 9 presets to 3 (vertical ignored), Align wires 3 of 13 (no to-Page/Margin, gridlines, multi-object distribute), Wrap omits 4 items (incl. the real Move-with-Text/Fix-Position OOXML toggles), Rotate lacks arbitrary-angle. All are image-only (no shapes/text boxes). One spec extends the arrange bridge verbs + menus + the More-Layout-Options dialog across the group.

buckets.name: Page-setup & break fidelity
controls: Layout › Margins (true mirror margins; per-side Custom dialog)
Layout › Size (orientation preserved on paper change)
buckets.rationale: Export is faithful for the common case but three Word behaviors diverge: Mirrored writes symmetric lr instead of w:mirrorMargins+inside/outside, choosing a paper size silently forces portrait, and Ctrl+Enter inserts an empty pageBreakBefore paragraph instead of splitting the paragraph at the caret (text after caret should move to the next page). One page-setup spec covers mirror margins, the per-side Custom Margins dialog, orientation preservation, and mid-paragraph split.

buckets.name: Design galleries collapse to one output (themes/style-set/fonts)
controls: Design › Themes (active checkmark stuck on Office)
buckets.rationale: Two real wrong-output bugs that share the redefine-named-styles path: themeUpdates()/redefineNamedStyles never enumerate the linked Heading*Char character styles, so headings keep the old major-font theme binding (body changes, headings don't) for both Themes and Fonts; and Style Set's name→preset map has none of the 9 gallery names, so all 9 produce identical Normal spacing. Plus the cosmetic checkmark stuck on Office (currentDocFont reads an unwritten --doc-font). One Design spec fixes the Char-style stripping, populates the style-set table, and tracks last-applied for the tick.

buckets.name: References gallery/dialog fidelity (TOC/notes/bibliography/cross-ref)
controls: References › Table of Contents (Auto 1/2 caption dropped; Manual harvests headings)
References › Style (pre-bibliography pick lost but toasts success)
References › Insert Table of Figures (re-insert stacks; no dialog)
buckets.rationale: The TOC/footnote/citation engines are substantial and the cores work, so this is a fidelity/wrong-output cleanup over one subsystem: drop the literal note seeds that leak into export (BUG-010), wire the Custom-TOC leader + Automatic-Table captions, filter note navigation by type, stop dropping the bibliography title + de-dupe re-inserts (BUG-031/062), persist citation style pre-bibliography, and fix the cross-reference 'page number' field (PAGEREF vs the \p above/below collision — a fork edit). One References fidelity spec.

buckets.name: References dialog completeness (Mark Entry / Caption / Index / ToA)
buckets.area: References › Index / Captions / Table of Authorities
controls: References › Mark Entry (no dialog; silent no-op without selection)
References › Insert Caption (no Position/New-Label/format; attaches to caret not object)
References › Insert Table of Authorities (no ToA dialog)
buckets.rationale: These fields work mechanically but lack their defining Word dialogs: Mark Entry silently no-ops with no selection and offers no Main/Subentry/cross-ref/page-range/Mark-All (the core of indexing UX), Caption has no position/label-management/format chooser and attaches to the caret not the selected object, and Index/ToA insert with zero options dialog. One spec adds the four reference dialogs on top of the existing field engines.

buckets.name: Header/footer/page-number gallery surfacing
controls: Insert › Header (5 ribbon items collapse to one text dialog)
Insert › Page Number (missing Page Margins position + Format Page Numbers)
buckets.rationale: Header/footer text round-trips (002, Word-COM validated) and page-number inserts a real PAGE field, but the Insert dropdowns collapse Word's 5 gallery items (Built-in gallery / More / Edit / Remove / Save Selection) into a single textarea, and Page Number omits Page-Margins position and Format Page Numbers (numbering format/start-at). One spec surfaces the galleries + the Format Page Numbers dialog over the working bridge. (The freshly-inserted-field-paints-0 issue is a known fork-gated paint gap noted separately.)

buckets.name: Insert text galleries: Cover Page designs + Date field static option
controls: Insert › Cover Page (6 designs collapse to one generic block)
buckets.rationale: Two wired-but-wrong inserts: xeCoverPage ignores the chosen design name and always emits the same 3-paragraph stub (all 6 gallery designs identical), and the Date & Time dialog's 'Update automatically' checkbox is never read so it always inserts an updating field instead of a static snapshot when unchecked. One spec gives the 6 cover designs distinct formatted layouts and honors the date checkbox.

buckets.name: Home font dialog & character-formatting edge cases
controls: Home › Font dialog (Small/All caps, Scale, Spacing, Position no-op + toast)
Home › Underline dropdown (no More Underlines / Underline Color)
Home › Shading (floods paragraph on sub-paragraph selection)
buckets.rationale: The Font dialog presents Caps/Scale/Spacing/Position controls that update only the preview then drop on OK with a notifyBlocked toast (no w:smallCaps/caps/spacing/position/w emitted) — a lying advanced tab. Grouped with the related character-formatting fidelity bugs: the font-size combo silently clamps typed 8–96 (fork minMax) vs Word 1–1638, Increase-Font ceilings at 72, underline color is unreachable anywhere, and Shading always writes pPr/w:shd (paragraph) even on a sub-paragraph selection where Word applies rPr/w:shd to the run. One Home-font spec restores the advanced-effects export, widens the size clamp, and scopes shading to the selection.

buckets.name: Home editing/marks fidelity (Show/Hide, Change Case, Sort, Highlight)
controls: Home › Show/Hide ¶ (only pilcrow; no space/tab/break marks; paged paint unverified)
Home › Text Highlight Color (full palette instead of 15-keyword set → non-keyword becomes w:shd shading)
Home › Change Case (Sentence-case/toggle edge cases; no Shift+F3 cycle)
Home › Sort (Date sorts numerically not chronologically)
Home › Text Effects Outline (width presets pass currentColor → invalid srgbClr dropped by Word)
buckets.rationale: Lower-severity Home fidelity gaps that don't share a single fix but are all 'Home looks right, behaves slightly wrong': Show/Hide draws only the pilcrow (no dots/arrows/break marks, and paged-paint match is unverified), the highlighter uses the full color palette so any non-keyword swatch silently becomes character shading with different semantics, Change-Case has locale-naive toggle + sentence-case regex edge cases and no Shift+F3, Sort treats dates as numbers, and the Text-Effects width presets emit an invalid 'currentColor' outline that Word drops on open. One Home-polish spec.

buckets.name: Draw tool fidelity & pressed-state
buckets.area: Draw › Pens / Tools / Convert / Insert / Replay
controls: Draw › Drawing toggle (button never latches; menu stuck on 'Start Drawing')
Draw › Pens Gallery (active highlight always first tile; no pen-options flyout)
Draw › Add Pen (no opacity, no persistence, Action Pen decorative)
Draw › Drawing Canvas (inline rect, not wpc:wpc; ink not grouped)
Draw › Ink Replay (no scrub UI; DOM-order not timestamps)
buckets.rationale: Ink mode functions but the Draw tab is riddled with wrong pressed-state UI (Drawing/pen-gallery latch reads WC.Draw.pen/.enabled which are never reassigned) and partial tool semantics (Select/Lasso are ink-only with no marquee/move, the Drawing Canvas is a plain inline rect not a real canvas group). One Draw spec fixes the state plumbing (TOGGLE_MAP/state rules + actually setting WC.Draw.pen/.enabled) and rounds out the selection/canvas behavior. Niche surface, hence P3.

buckets.name: Mailings recipient & job-control UX
controls: Mailings › Go to Record (generic spinner: wrong icon, step 6, 'pt' unit, default 0, no sync-back)
Mailings › Envelopes (manual-break div dropped; no envelope page size)
Mailings › Labels (Full-page checkbox inert; no Avery geometry)
Mailings › Update Labels (stub; propagation now feasible)
Mailings › Edit Recipient List (aliases entry grid; no sort/filter/dedupe)
Mailings › Check for Errors (one-shot vs 3-mode dialog)
buckets.rationale: The remaining Mailings issues are UX/layout rather than the P1 export-correctness set: the Go-to-Record spinner is mis-rendered by the generic renderSpinner (BUG-021) and never syncs after navigation, Envelopes/Labels lose their page separation/geometry (the manual-break div the paged importer can't parse — same family as Finish&Merge but lower stakes), the inert Full-page-of-same-label checkbox, and Update-Labels/Edit-Recipient-List remain stubs/aliases now mechanically buildable. One Mailings-UX spec.

buckets.name: View zoom/ruler/navigation approximations
controls: View › Multiple Pages (fixed fit-2; no grid chooser)
View › Ruler (static decorative; no interactive markers; no geometry)
View › Navigation Pane (checkbox never reflects open state; no Pages/Results tabs)
View › Immersive Reader (minimal subset; tooltip over-promises)
buckets.rationale: Functional-but-approximate View features that share the theme of ignoring real section geometry / lying pressed-state: One Page and Multiple Pages use a hardcoded 1056px page height (wrong for A4/Legal/Landscape), the Ruler is static and non-interactive, the Navigation Pane works but its checkbox never latches and it lacks Pages-thumbnail/search tabs, and Immersive Reader is a small subset of what its tooltip advertises. One View-polish spec drives zoom/ruler from real geometry and fixes the nav-pane latch.

buckets.name: Word Options & document-default persistence
controls: File › Options pane (read-only; no editable settings)
Design › Set as Default (pure toast, no persistence)
File › Close (replaces doc with New instead of closing)
File › Recent list (pin / clear-unpinned UI missing; backend half-wired)
buckets.rationale: A small persistence/settings cluster: Word Options is purely informational (default font/AutoCorrect have real impact but aren't editable), Set-as-Default neither persists nor is read by New Document, File→Close silently does New (semantics wrong, not data-loss since confirmDiscard runs), and the Recent list lacks pin/clear-unpinned UI though the recent:clear backend exists. One spec adds a minimal persistence layer (localStorage/template) + fixes Close semantics.

buckets.name: Review proofing & markup depth
controls: Review › Spelling and Grammar (routes to Editor pane; split items identical; no step-through modal)
Review › Show Markup (Specific People/Highlight Updates/Other Authors stubbed; no multi-author model)
Review › Show Comments / Filter All Markup / Hide Ink (render-verify + interim routing)
buckets.rationale: The Review subsystem is substantively built (real local Editor/spell/grammar loop, real track-chrome hide toggles) but shallow vs Word: no selection-scoped word count, the classic step-through Spelling modal is replaced by the Editor pane (and the split's two items are identical), the thesaurus dictionary is tiny, accessibility/markup have unwired split items and no multi-author model, and Language doesn't write per-run w:lang. One Review-depth spec deepens proofing coverage and wires the missing markup/accessibility items.

buckets.name: Real-object insert subsystems (Shapes/SmartArt/Chart/Signature/OLE/Equation)
controls: Insert › Shapes (full picker, every click is a no-op toast; VectorShape fork ext exists)
Insert › Chart (dialog computes SVG but xeChart no-op; chart ext render-only)
Insert › Object (Create New OLE no-op; Text from File works)
Insert › Equation (Cambria-Math styled text not m:oMath; Alt+= unwired)
buckets.rationale: These are stubs behind complete-looking pickers — Shapes is the most misleading (full 8-category gallery, zero document change) but a VectorShape fork extension already exists so a real wps:sp path is feasible; SmartArt/Chart/Signature similarly render dialogs over no-op bridge verbs, Equation is a documented styled-text deviation (not real OOXML m:oMath), and OLE/Stock/Online/Convert-Text are host-runtime/cloud stubs. Grouped as the 'make insert objects real' epic; P3 because most need new render/diagram/OLE subsystems and several are honest degrades, but Shapes is the priority within the bucket.

buckets.name: Honest cloud/ML/runtime degrade stubs (no action)
controls: Home › Dictate / Sensitivity / Add-ins / Reuse Files
Draw › Ink to Shape / Ink to Math
View › Synchronous Scrolling / Reset Window Position / Switch Windows / Macros
File › Share / Feedback / Info Protect-Inspect-Version History
buckets.rationale: These are intentional, documented honest-degrade stubs that toast and make no document change — they require cloud services (Translator, Researcher, Smart Lookup, Sharing, Sensitivity/Purview), ML (ink recognition), a VBA runtime (Macros), Office.js (Add-ins), or multi-window/document backends (Switch Windows, Sync Scrolling) that are out of scope for a single-window local clone. Tracked together as a 'known-not-implemented' register so they aren't re-triaged each pass; no spec work expected unless scope changes (notably the stale Dictate/Sensitivity doc claims should be corrected). Includes the lowest-severity S5 items.
